project.name: ClearHealth
project.purpose: Multi-tenant HIPAA-compliant appointment platform. Monorepo with packages/api, packages/web, packages/shared, packages/notifications, Prisma schema, infrastructure, and docs.

module_map.purpose: Express API server — endpoints, middleware (auth, audit, PII guard), services (encryption, insurance, notifications), and business logic. All routes are tenant-scoped, audit-logged, and PII-filtered.
Next.js 14 App Router frontend — React server components, shadcn/ui design system, Tailwind CSS. Server-component-first architecture ensuring patient data never reaches client state.
Shared contract layer between frontend, backend, and notifications. Contains TypeScript types, Zod schemas, and shared interfaces including notification payload interfaces.

Delivery-only service for SMS and email appointment reminders. Receives pre-tokenized payloads from the backend containing delivery address, template ID, and display-safe template variables. Never accesses patient records, never receives patient IDs or raw PII.
Prisma schema, migrations, and seed data. Defines the multi-tenant data model with PII field annotations and encryption markers.
Terraform configs, deployment scripts, backup and key rotation scripts.
CI/CD pipelines — lint, typecheck, test, security audit, PII scan gates, and deployment workflows.
Project documentation, API reference, architecture docs.

required_artifacts.purpose: Project overview, setup instructions, and architecture summary
Environment variable template with no real secrets

principles.name: hipaa-compliance
principles.statement: HIPAA compliance and patient data security always win. No design decision, performance optimization, or feature deadline overrides patient data protection.

principles.name: tenant-isolation
principles.statement: Tenant isolation is inviolable. No data may leak across clinic boundaries. Every data access path must be scoped by tenantId.

principles.name: audit-completeness
principles.statement: Every patient data access is logged through audit middleware with no exceptions. The audit trail must be complete and tamper-evident.

principles.name: type-safety
principles.statement: Strict TypeScript everywhere. No use of 'any' types. Zod schemas validate all data at API boundaries. The type system is a safety net, not a suggestion.

principles.name: test-coverage
principles.statement: Nothing ships without tests. New endpoints require test coverage. All changes must pass the full CI gate before landing.

principles.name: code-clarity
principles.statement: Code clarity over cleverness. Prefer readable, explicit implementations over terse or abstract patterns.

sensitivity_tiers.description: Patient data, PHI, authentication, encryption, PII handling. Any change requires explicit human approval.
Infrastructure, CI/CD, deployment configs. Requires human approval.
Shared types, cross-boundary work, notifications service. Requires review before merge.
Feature work within owned scope, documentation, tests, routine refactoring.
